focus_areas: Real-world light field depth estimation challenges (non-Lambertian, multi-domain)
Limitations of existing EPI-based and learning-based methods
Proposed physics-driven dual-mask architecture and contributions
must_include: motivation_hook: Traditional light field depth estimation fails in real-world scenarios due to non-Lambertian reflections and domain shifts
contribution_claim: Bulleted list of 3 contributions (Physics-based 3-layer decomposition, Geometric Dual-Mask architecture, SOTA multi-domain performance with quantitative evidence)
Clear gap statement highlighting the failure of linear EPI assumptions on complex materials and the overfitting of single-domain models

Introduction.target_emphasis: To establish the critical failure of traditional linear EPI assumptions in real-world multi-domain scenarios and introduce the physics-guided dual-mask solution.

focus_areas: Light field depth estimation (EPI-based and learning-based)
Non-Lambertian and reflection handling in light fields

Categorical subheadings for different approaches (by_approach organization)

should_avoid: Detailed explanations of standard CNN/Transformer architectures not specific to light fields
First-person singular narrative (use 'we' or passive voice)
Related Work.target_emphasis: To systematically categorize prior art and expose the critical gaps in handling non-Lambertian materials and multi-domain shifts, justifying the need for physical priors and dual-mask routing.

focus_areas: Three-layer angular signal decomposition and geometric material classification
Geometric Dual-Mask unified depth estimation architecture
